Level_1_Needs_work: No persistence boundary; reporting hard-coded; mocks in the production path.
Level_2_Getting_there: Some abstraction but store/registry split unclear or reporting disconnected.
Level_3_Acceptable: Clear pipeline stages; persistence abstraction; reporting reads real store data.
Level_4_Outstanding: Level 3 plus coherent layering for future HA (even if single-node today).

Level_1_Needs_work: Idempotency broken; illegal status transitions; failures corrupt state silently.
Level_2_Getting_there: Partial idempotency; status model shaky; failure behaviour undocumented.
Level_3_Acceptable: Idempotency proven; coherent statuses; failure behaviour documented and tested.
Level_4_Outstanding: Level 3 plus transactional boundaries or concurrency story where justified.

Level_1_Needs_work: No duplicate-key or failure tests; mvn test fails.
Level_2_Getting_there: Limited tests; reporting not validated on fixtures.
Level_3_Acceptable: Duplicate-key tests; failure injections; reporting validated on fixtures.
Level_4_Outstanding: Level 3 plus broad regression suite for persistence edge cases.

Level_1_Needs_work: No DDL discipline; data dir chaos; reports not reproducible from clean DB.
Level_2_Getting_there: Basic persistence; weak runbook or hygiene.
Level_3_Acceptable: DDL/schema artefact; data/ hygiene; honest aggregates from persisted rows.
Level_4_Outstanding: Level 3 plus runbook for wipe/rebuild and incident narrative quality.

Level_2_Getting_there: Thin notes on idempotency or schema.
Level_3_Acceptable: Incident narrative + schema/runbook; instructor can reproduce reports locally.
Level_4_Outstanding: Level 3 plus crisp ops story for duplicate POST storm and recovery.
